description: Photos show damage pattern (e.g. paint transfer from another vehicle) that contradicts a claimed single-vehicle event.

pattern: Repeated claims by same policyholder in short window
description: Two or more claims within 12 months — especially on similar areas of the vehicle — raises moral-hazard risk.
Theft or collision reports filed more than 48 hours after the alleged incident, with no plausible reason.

pattern: Missing supporting documents on a high-value claim
description: Claim amount > $10,000 without repair estimate, photos, or police report.
Descriptions lacking time, location, weather, or witness specifics can indicate fabrication.

pattern: Repair estimate far above market norm
description: Submitted invoice exceeds typical regional repair cost for the described damage by > 50%.
Amount conveniently sits just below max_coverage — pattern flag for inflated claims.

pattern: Vehicle moved or repaired before assessment
description: Claimant cannot produce the damaged vehicle for inspection or has already repaired it.
